mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate over several months directly signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or falling UNRATE periods do not produce significantly positive returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near zero (≤0.25%), monetary policy is maximally accommodative, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS≤0.25, forward 20-day returns are negative more than 50% of occurrences or average return not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: The joint combination of low and stable consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates that monetary accommodation is not yet generating inflationary pressures, creating a 'Goldilocks' macro environment that supports equity prices. The joint signal is stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL * FEDFUNDS) does not add predictive power over individual series in a walk-forward regression (p>0.10) or portfolio backtest Sharpe ratio < 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals a robust labor market recovery that will translate into increased consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE>5.5 and PAYEMS is rising, forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or the signal's rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strength in residential construction and broader consumer demand, which boosts economic growth and corporate earnings, leading to positive short-term equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not produce positive average forward 5-day returns with a Sharpe ratio > 0.5 over any 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing factory output and overall economic activity, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO do not lead to positive average forward 60-day returns with a significant t-statistic (p<0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are rising concurrently with subdued consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL), real consumer demand is growing without overheating, which is a strong positive signal for equity markets. The interaction of demand strength and price stability is more informative than either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint signal (RSAFS increase with CPIAUCSL stability) does not outperform a simple RSAFS-only signal in walk-forward rank IC or portfolio return attribution.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals the onset of a monetary tightening cycle, which increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows and reduces valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25, forward 20-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).
